basic_info.headline: I aspire to build useful, safe and fast tools

experience.title: Entrepreneur
experience.description:
As an Entrepreneur In Residence at AI2's Incubator, I talked to customers, built prototypes, pitched to investors, raised a VC round, and co-founded a company called Augment. It's a productivity tool that integrates deeply into the way you already work. I built the software platform from event processing, data synthesis to the client foundations. After a year, I quit to enjoy a summer off from working.

The company was later bought by private equity, and pivoted in a new direction.

experience.title: Manager
experience.description: After 5 years of building the Timeline system and team as a manager, I followed my passion for visual story telling and joined Computational Photography. I built the engineering team and established the mission of innovating new ways for people to capture their moments anywhere and bring them to life as shareable experiences. We shipped image enhancement algorithms for new hardware products, and new interactive media formats such as 3D Photos, 360 Photos, Rotatable Photos used by millions. And innovated new experiences for social in AR, video storytelling and living portraits.

experience.title: Engineer, then Manager
experience.description: I built infrastructure and product for the Identity organization. I scaled the Timeline system to billions of users supporting dozens of product organizations and did it while increasing efficiency to save 10s of millions of dollars in CapEx. I innovated and shipped products based on memories that became cultural icons (e.g. Year In Review, Personalized Videos, Profile) and touched billions of people.

experience.title: Engineer
experience.description:
I helped launch Wavii, a service that creates feeds about topics in the world through natural language data mining.

On the frontend, I built the user analytics system, weekly digests, Facebook open-graph integration and the feed rendering system. I did research on recommendations algorithms and user behaviors including conducting numerous A/B tests.

On the backend, I built and scaled up a real-time news crawler that replaced and exceeded our vendor service at 10% of the cost. I also built a low-latency high-throughput data service for the feed.

On the side, I designed and built experiments like Politics, Olympics and Stream. These and other unpublished experiments explored new system architectures and ways of visualizing our data.
